flashcards.front: What does Big O notation describe?
flashcards.back: Upper bound of algorithm's growth rate, describing worst-case scenario performance.

flashcards.front: What's the time complexity of a single loop iterating n times?

flashcards.back: Time: CPU operations required. Space: Memory allocation needed.
Cubic time - grows proportionally to cube of input size.
Constant time - execution time unaffected by input size.
Best: Optimal input scenario. Average: Expected performance across random inputs.

flashcards.front: What's the complexity of binary search?
flashcards.back: O(log n) - halves search space each iteration.

flashcards.front: Why ignore constants in Big O?
flashcards.back: Focuses on growth rate, not absolute performance metrics.

flashcards.front: What's O(n log n) complexity used for?
flashcards.back: Efficient sorting algorithms like Merge Sort and Quick Sort.
Omega notation - lower bound of algorithm's performance.
Multiply complexities: O(n²) for two nested O(n) loops.

flashcards.front: What's space complexity of recursive Fibonacci?
flashcards.back: O(n) - call stack depth proportional to input.
